relevant_cases.verbatim_excerpt: A 69-year-old man presented with a 1-day history of chest pain. He was diagnosed with a non-ST-segment elevation myocardial infarction on the basis of elevated high-sensit
relevant_cases.relevance_notes: Post-PCI complication (transradial coronary intervention) with stenting. Developed marked hypotension (70/20 mmHg), tachycardia, chest discomfort. Hemoglobin drop from 102 to 81 g/L. Features match target: post-procedural hypotension refractory to fluids, access-related vascular injury (subclavian dissection/perforation), hemoglobin drop.
